Title: Natural variation of WBR7 confers rice high yield and quality by modulating sucrose supply in sink organs

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Sucrose synthase family; GT-4 glycosyltransferase domain
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Sucrose metabolism; starch and sucrose metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Sucrose cleavage/reversible sucrose synthase step affecting carbon allocation to sink organs
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream entry/control step in sucrose utilization and carbon partitioning
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reversibly catalyzes conversion between sucrose + UDP and fructose + UDP-glucose; in this study mainly functions in sucrose decomposition
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and controls sucrose allocation toward storage component accumulation in endosperm and starch accumulation in anthers
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major transport assimilate providing carbon and energy for grain filling, storage compound synthesis, pollen maturation and germination
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher-activity WBR7(G)/BL130 allele promotes sucrose decomposition in conducting organs and endosperm, increases white-belly rate, and reduces sucrose supply to sink organs; lower-activity WBR7(A)/J23B allele decreases white-belly rate and improves yield and quality
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Barely expressed in seedlings; gradually increased in branch, leaf sheath, stem, flag leaf, husk, panicle and endosperm; also expressed in anther
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in branch, leaf sheath, stem, flag leaf, husk, panicle, endosperm and anther during reproductive stages; low in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field growth in Wuhan and Lingshui; grain filling stages after flowering; 13C-sucrose feeding assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional SNP A/G at exon 11 (+3114 bp) causes E541K substitution in the GT-4 domain; WBR7A from Jin23B has significantly lower sucrose decomposition activity than WBR7G from BL130; the weak allele arose during indica domestication and is absent from japonica according to this paper
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher-activity allele causes increased grain chalkiness/white-belly, reduced milled rice rate, reduced seed setting rate and reduced yield per plant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; near-isogenic lines; complementation; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; recombinant protein enzyme assay; sucrose enzyme activity assays; qRT-PCR; RNA-seq; metabolomics; 13C-sucrose feeding; phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of 4 DAF endosperm; central carbon metabolomics at 8 DAF; accession numbers not provided
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for sucrose allocation and sink-source carbon partitioning; rate-limiting status not explicitly established
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: The weak WBR7A allele is a valuable breeding target for reducing white-belly, improving milled rice quality, increasing seed setting and yield; allele replacement/editing of WBR7G to WBR7A is recommended, especially in indica and japonica backgrounds lacking WBR7A
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native WBR7 promoter (2 kb) used for complementation; type: native/endogenous
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WBR7 was map-based cloned as LOC_Os07g42490 encoding SUS3 and acts as a positive regulator of rice white-belly rate. 2. A functional exon-11 A/G SNP causing E541K in the GT-4 domain reduces sucrose decomposition activity in the J23B allele. 3. Lower-activity WBR7A decreases sucrose metabolism in conducting organs, increases sucrose supply to endosperm and anthers, enhances storage component accumulation and pollen performance, and improves grain quality and yield.
